result.summary: Mapped AuthZed’s public integration surfaces: 2 HTTP APIs (SpiceDB HTTP API and tenant-specific AuthZed Cloud API), 2 MCP servers (AuthZed MCP Server and SpiceDB Dev MCP Server), and the zed CLI; confirmed bearer-token auth for the Cloud API and left the other surfaces’ auth as undocumented/unknown from public docs.

authzed_cloud_bearer.label: AuthZed Cloud API bearer token
authzed_cloud_bearer.setup: The [AuthZed Cloud API announcement](https://authzed.com/blog/introducing-the-authzed-cloud-api) says the API is in Tech Preview and that you must contact your account team to request access. After access is enabled, AuthZed provides credentials for the API. Use the issued token as a `Bearer` token, and send the required API version header `X-API-Version` alongside requests.

requiredHeaders.name: X-API-Version
requiredHeaders.description: Version header shown as required in AuthZed's Cloud API announcement example.

variables.name: YOUR_AUTHZED_DEDICATED_ENDPOINT
variables.description: Tenant-specific dedicated endpoint hostname used to form the API base URL.

packages.runtimeHint: Go-based standalone CLI distributed via GitHub releases and package managers.
